name: Gibraltar
summary: Gibraltar is a Western European common-law British Overseas Territory — structurally distinctive globally as the only state-level entity directly bordering Spain with British common-law framework, and post-Brexit as the only BOT formerly in the European Union (until 31 January 2020). Family-law framework operates under the Children Act 2009 (drawing on English Children Act 1989 model), the Matrimonial Causes Act 1962, the Marriage Act 2010 (including same-sex marriage), and English common-law inherited principles. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Children Act 2009 Part II. The Court of Appeal of Gibraltar is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction was retained with the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council in London. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Supreme Court of Gibraltar (Family Division) and Magistrates' Court. Psychology profession is regulated through the Gibraltar Health Authority. Gibraltar is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child principle codified in Children Act 2009 s. 1. Gibraltar is a Hague Convention 1980 party via UK territorial extension effective 1 August 1986. The Gibraltar protocol of the UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement is under negotiation post-Brexit for cross-border arrangements.

statutory_framework.title: Children Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children Act drawing on English Children Act 1989 model codifying welfare-of-the-child principle (s. 1), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions.

statutory_framework.title: Matrimonial Causes Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal matrimonial-causes statute drawing on English Matrimonial Causes Act model.

statutory_framework.title: Marriage Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Marriage Act including same-sex marriage provisions.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Gibraltar
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Judicial Committee of the Privy Council
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Gibraltar.

professional_regulators.name: Gibraltar Health Authority
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Gibraltarian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Capture of Gibraltar + War-of-Spanish-Succession framework
key_developments.description: Capture of Gibraltar by Anglo-Dutch forces 4 August 1704 during War of the Spanish Succession — substantively distinctive Western European British-territorial-acquisition framework. Foundational pre-Treaty-of-Utrecht framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Utrecht + Gibraltar-British-cession framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Utrecht 11 April 1713 substantively distinctive globally Spanish-Gibraltar-British-cession framework — 313+ year Gibraltar-British-Crown framework persisting through 21st century. Substantive Anglo-Spanish-territorial-dispute framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Gibraltar sovereignty referendum 1967 + UK-status-confirmation framework
key_developments.description: Gibraltar sovereignty referendum 10 September 1967 — substantively distinctive Western European 99.64% in favour of remaining British framework (vs Spanish sovereignty). Substantive self-determination framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by UK to Gibraltar effective 1 August 1986 — establishing Western European Hague Convention jurisdiction framework within UK BOT framework.

key_developments.title: UK ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + BOT extension
key_developments.description: United Kingdom ratified the UNCRC on 16 December 1991 with extension to Gibraltar — establishing welfare-of-the-child substantive doctrine within UK-derivative common-law framework applicable to Gibraltar.

key_developments.title: Gibraltar second-sovereignty-referendum 2002 + 99% in favour of remaining British framework
key_developments.description: Gibraltar second-sovereignty referendum 7 November 2002 — substantively distinctive Western European 98.97% in favour of remaining British framework against Anglo-Spanish-joint-sovereignty proposal. Substantive self-determination framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Gibraltar 2006 + constitutional-modernisation framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Gibraltar 2006 adopted 14 December 2006 by referendum — substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing modernised constitutional framework. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence within UK BOT framework.

key_developments.title: Children Act 2009 + Marriage Act 2010 + same-sex-marriage framework
key_developments.description: Federal Children Act 2009 enacted drawing on English Children Act 1989 model codifying welfare-of-the-child principle (s. 1), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions. Federal Marriage Act 2010 including same-sex marriage provisions substantively significant Western European Catholic-BOT post-marriage-equality framework.

key_developments.title: Brexit + only-BOT-formerly-in-EU framework
key_developments.description: UK (including Gibraltar) left the European Union effective 31 January 2020 — substantively distinctive globally only-BOT-formerly-in-EU framework. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework with Spain affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory. Gibraltar Protocol negotiations 2020-onwards substantively significant cross-border-jurisdiction-framework.

key_developments.title: Picardo premiership + Care Agency framework + Gibraltar-EU Treaty negotiations consolidation
key_developments.description: Fabian Picardo (GSLP) continuing-premiership 2011-onwards framework consolidation through 2023. Care Agency institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Children Act 2009 + Marriage Act 2010 framework. Gibraltar-EU Treaty negotiations consolidation through 2023 substantively significant cross-border family-law adjudication framework with Spain. Substantively significant Mediterranean BOT institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Court of Appeal of Gibraltar + JCPC — welfare-of-the-child substantive register + Gibraltar-EU-Treaty-framework negotiation context
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Gibraltar and Judicial Committee of the Privy Council continue to develop welfare-of-the-child jurisprudence under Children Act 2009 s. 1 + Marriage Act 2010 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within continuing Gibraltar-EU-Treaty-framework negotiation context. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Gibraltar operates a common-law framework drawing on English-law model with British Overseas Territory status — places Gibraltar in the BOT cluster.
Post-Brexit position is structurally distinctive globally — only BOT formerly in the EU; Gibraltar protocol under negotiation for cross-border arrangements with Spain.
Judicial Committee of the Privy Council retention as final appellate court is structurally consistent with British Overseas Territory framework.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via UK territorial extension reflects BOT Hague jurisdiction status.

sources.title: Judicial Committee of the Privy Council

editorial_notes: Gibraltar jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Capture-of-Gibraltar-1704-to-Gibraltar-EU-Treaty trajectory: 1704-Capture-of-Gibraltar + 1713-Treaty-of-Utrecht + 1967-Gibraltar-sovereignty-referendum + 1986-Hague-Convention-1980-territorial-extension + 1991-UK-UNCRC-+-BOT-extension + 2002-Gibraltar-second-sovereignty-referendum + 2006-Constitution-of-Gibraltar + 2009-Children-Act-+-Marriage-Act-2010 + 2020-Brexit-+-only-BOT-formerly-in-EU + 2024-Court-of-Appeal-+-JCPC-+-Gibraltar-EU-Treaty.
Common-law Western European BOT (Children Act 2009 + Marriage Act 2010 + JCPC final-appellate + Hague via UK territorial extension 1986). Post-Brexit position distinctive globally.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under Children Act 2009 s. 1 + Marriage Act 2010 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Western-European + common-law + BOT-cluster + only-state-level-entity-directly-bordering-Spain-with-British-common-law-globally-distinctive + only-BOT-formerly-in-EU-distinctive + JCPC-final-appellate + Hague-via-UK-territorial-extension + Capture-of-Gibraltar-1704-+-Treaty-of-Utrecht-1713-313-years + Gibraltar-sovereignty-referendum-1967-99.64-percent-+-2002-98.97-percent-British-distinctive + Constitution-of-Gibraltar-2006 + Children-Act-2009-+-Marriage-Act-2010-same-sex + Brexit-2020-Protocol + Gibraltar-Protocol-EU-Treaty-negotiations-2020-onwards clusters within the corpus.
